title: Founder Signal AI Pattern Recognition Model
goal: Identify pre-funding founder traits that correlate with future ultra-success outcomes and use them in seed-stage diligence.
company_value_methodology: Public companies: use latest market cap snapshot from primary quote pages (Google Finance preferred) and refresh regularly.
Private companies: use latest publicly disclosed valuation from credible financial reporting (Reuters, WSJ, major financial media, filings).
Merged entities: use reported combined valuation only when explicitly disclosed; otherwise keep companies separate.
If private valuation is undisclosed, store value as null instead of guessing; include note and source.
Each company entry must include as_of date, source_url, and confidence to make staleness and reliability transparent.

Inflation policy for future historical comparisons: current founders use today's nominal market value; historical founders use the total company value as of retirement/death, then inflation-adjust that total into current dollars before cross-cohort comparisons.
methodology: Input data: pre-cutoff fact libraries for historical founders from DB or JSON logs.
Feature construction: category counts, source density, timing, and interaction terms (technical x risk, AI x curiosity).
Unsupervised step: SentenceTransformer embeddings + K-means to detect founder archetype clusters.
Supervised step: XGBoost classifier on labeled success vs control founders to rank predictive signals.
MVP strategy: with only 3 successful examples, synthetic control profiles are generated to allow supervised fitting; this is a bootstrap baseline until real negatives are added.

companies.notes: Latest broadly cited public estimate appears stale.
No reliable current public valuation disclosure.

findings: family_hardship_count: higher in exemplar founders by 6.92 on average (importance 1.453).
attribute_count: higher in exemplar founders by 27.22 on average (importance 1.379).
source_density: higher in exemplar founders by 27.22 on average (importance 1.379).
decision_making_count: higher in exemplar founders by 1.57 on average (importance 1.057).
education_count: higher in exemplar founders by 10.12 on average (importance 1.028).
early_technical_proficiency_count: higher in exemplar founders by 2.06 on average (importance 0.997).
interview_questions: What high-conviction technical project did you build before external funding, and why did you choose it?
Describe a major pre-funding risk you took that could have failed badly. Why was it worth it?
Which early hardship most changed your operating behavior as a founder?
How quickly do you make irreversible decisions under uncertainty, and what evidence do you require?
What ideas in AI do you study deeply beyond your current product roadmap?
Which mentors or peers materially changed your trajectory before founding?
Tell me about a time you persisted through failure when quitting was rational.
What contrarian belief do you hold about your market that most smart people disagree with?
How do you balance speed versus quality when technical debt and customer pressure conflict?
If you had zero capital for 18 months, what is your exact build-and-go-to-market plan?
